channel.title: The Grant Desk - UK, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: UK, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Corridor: DAISY CHAIN Artist Commission (Adur, West Sussex)
item.description:
Organisation: Corridor Projects

Location: Adur and Worthing, West Sussex, UK. Open to a socially-engaged artist based in the South East of England working with moving image / lens-based media.

Award: GBP 4,200 artist fee (inclusive of VAT) paid in instalments, plus travel, and an additional budget for materials for creative production, workshops and exhibition outputs (e.g. print). No application fee.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027: workshops September-January, post-production to February 2027, UK and Czech tour March-July 2027

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

Corridor seeks a socially-engaged artist based in the South East of England working with moving image / lens-based media for DAISY CHAIN, marking the 60th anniversary of Vera Chytilova's experimental film Daisies (1966). The commissioned artist will work with a group of LGBTQIA+ young people aged 15-26 in Adur (West Sussex) to co-create new artworks: a 2-3 minute moving image work and a series of 2D collage or mixed media artworks for public exhibition and screenings. DAISY CHAIN commissions six new artist moving image works overall (three England-based artists working with older women, young women and LGBTQIA+ youth; three artists in Czechia), developed in partnership with the National Film Museum in Prague (NaFilM) and Brighton-based videoclub, with results toured across England and Czechia in 2027. EXPECTATIONS: devise about eight hands-on workshops incorporating film, collage and mixed media (participants won't necessarily have an arts background); participate in 4-6 online peer sessions on collaborative practice led by artist Annis Joslin; complete exhibition-ready artworks on time and to budget; maintain a transparent dialogue on authorship with participants. Artists must have a participatory practice and experience of working creatively with young people. Note: to create a safe space, workshops will not be publicly labelled as LGBTQIA+. FEE: GBP 4,200 incl. VAT plus travel and a materials budget. TIMELINE: shortlist notified 7 August, online interviews 12 August 2026; launch event in Adur/Worthing March 2027. DEADLINE: 10am, 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Modern Art Oxford: Collaborative Micro-commissions Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Modern Art Oxford

Location: Oxford, UK. Open to collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex).

Award: GBP 1,000 artist fee per selected collective, plus up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship from the Modern Art Oxford team, four months of public exhibition display, professional installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, artwork transport support within the South East, peer networking, and inclusion in a public preview event. No application fee.

Duration: Production August to November 2026; install 23 November to 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027

Deadline: 26 Jul 2026

Modern Art Oxford marks its 60th anniversary with an open call for three collaborative micro-commissions responding to the question: how can collective practice/s help us to embrace uncertainty and complexity? Collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based across the South East of England are invited to propose new small-scale experimental works for a group exhibition in the Ground Floor Gallery, running alongside a major solo exhibition by Mike Nelson, who is also on the selection panel. Proposals may take the form of multidisciplinary research, site-responsive work, grassroots organising, performance, visual art, sound, poetry or other creative practices; ideas should respond to the theme of collective practice and uncertainty, explore collaborative approaches, experiment across disciplines, be achievable within the budget and timeline, and be original to this open call (building on existing research is fine). WHO: groups of two or more people, including established artist collectives, newly formed collaborations, interdisciplinary partnerships, informal or community-led groups, and family collaborations, based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex). SUPPORT: each of the three selected collectives receives a GBP 1,000 artist fee, up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship, four months of exhibition display, installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, and transport support within the South East. TIMELINE: online briefing and Q&A 15 June 2026 (email creativelearning@modernartoxford.org.uk for the link); selection confirmed 14 August 2026; production August to November 2026; preview party 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027. Apply via Google Forms; an information pack PDF and FAQ are on the call page. DEADLINE: Sunday 26 July 2026, 23:59.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Serpentine Galleries: Live Programmes Curator
item.description:
Organisation: Serpentine Galleries

Location: London, United Kingdom; hybrid working (3 days in office, 2 remote).

Award: GBP 45,000 per year. Permanent role (3-month probation). No application fee.

Duration: Permanent appointment; hybrid, 3 days office / 2 remote. 25 days annual leave rising to 30 after 5 years, plus 8 bank holidays.

Deadline: Closed 10 Jul 2026

Serpentine Galleries seeks an experienced interdisciplinary Live Programmes Curator to initiate, develop, and produce a year-round live programme that extends and activates the gallery's exhibitions and curatorial vision through talks, performances, participatory experiences, and cross-disciplinary live formats. Building on Serpentine's flagship strands (the annual Marathons, Park Nights, Serpentine Cinema, and Study Days), the curator will run a live programme alongside and through onsite exhibitions, working closely with the Programmes, Production, and Audience teams. RESPONSIBILITIES: conceive, curate, and deliver live events (talks, performances, workshops, screenings, new formats); develop formats that bring visual artists into dialogue with dance, music, film, literature, technology, design, and social practice; lead planning and delivery on time and on budget; and develop programming that attracts new, diverse, and multi-generational audiences onsite and online. REQUIREMENTS: significant experience curating and/or producing live programmes in a gallery, museum, festival, performance, or cultural context; a strong interdisciplinary curatorial approach across multiple artistic forms; excellent project management, budgeting, and scheduling; strong communication and collaboration; and deep knowledge of contemporary art and current cultural discourse. TERMS: permanent role (3-month probationary period); hybrid working (3 days office, 2 remote); 25 days annual leave rising to 30 after 5 years plus 8 bank holidays; auto-enrolment pension, season-ticket loan, and Cycle2Work scheme. SALARY: GBP 45,000 per annum. TO APPLY: submit a CV and cover letter addressing how your skills fit the role, examples of relevant experience, and what attracts you to Serpentine. DEADLINE: midnight, 10 July 2026; first-stage interviews w/c 20 July 2026, second-stage w/c 27 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Upe Foundation Curatorial Fellowship at Camden Art Centre 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Upe Foundation (in partnership with Camden Art Centre)

Location: London, United Kingdom. Full-time, in-person; relocation to London is required. Applicants must hold the right to work in the UK.

Award: Salary of GBP 30,960 per year. A full-time Assistant Curator role on a 12-month fixed-term contract, with the possibility of a further 6-month extension (18 months total) subject to formal review and mutual agreement.

Duration: 12-month fixed-term position starting Autumn 2026, with possible 6-month extension (18 months total).

Deadline: Closed 13 Jul 2026

Upe Foundation is launching the Upe Foundation Curatorial Fellowship at Camden Art Centre as part of its Fall 2026 Curatorial Fellowship Programme. The opportunity invites Baltic early-career curators to apply for a full-time curatorial role at Camden Art Centre, an institution internationally acclaimed for its rigorous exhibitions, residencies, events and learning programmes. The fellowship is a full-time, 12-month position starting Autumn 2026, with the possibility of a further 6-month extension (18 months total) upon review and mutual decision; relocation to London is required. ROLE: embedded within Camden Art Centre's Exhibitions Department, the Fellow works as Assistant Curator, supporting the development, coordination and delivery of the exhibitions programme and related projects and events, with practical experience across exhibition research, production, interpretation, publications, public programmes, loans, installation and documentation, and liaising with artists, galleries, lenders, partner organisations, writers and designers. The Fellow also shares research connected to Baltic and broader Central Eastern European artistic contexts. ELIGIBILITY: a demonstrated connection to or sustained engagement with the Baltic region (Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia, or their diasporas), which may include lived experience, heritage, working knowledge of a Baltic language, relevant research, or curatorial work with Baltic artists/institutions/contexts; early-career curatorial experience (freelance, self-organised, project-based or institutional); and a higher education qualification (BA, MA or equivalent) in curating, art history, visual culture or related disciplines. Desirable: working knowledge of Lithuanian, Latvian or Estonian; fluency in a second language; experience with Articheck and with CRM systems (particularly Salesforce). Applicants must hold the right to work in the UK (via the under-26 'new entrant' Skilled Worker route, the Creative Worker visa route, or existing right to work such as British citizenship or settled/pre-settled status). OFFER: GBP 30,960 salary, structured professional development within the Exhibitions team, and opportunities to build professional networks in London and internationally. Applications reviewed jointly by Upe Foundation and Camden Art Centre, with first-round online interviews and a second interview for finalists. The organizers strongly encourage applicants to write materials in their own words and avoid generic or AI-generated responses. TIMELINE: application deadline 12:00 noon UK time, Monday 13 July 2026; interviews 6 August 2026; final decision mid-August 2026; start Autumn 2026. Questions: recruitment@camdenartcentre.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creative Steps for Individuals 2026 (Arts Council of Wales)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Wales

Location: Wales, United Kingdom. For artists and creatives based in Wales.

Award: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs). Applicants can apply for up to 90% of the developmental cost; the remaining 10% must come from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both).

Duration: Supports a stage of professional development; allow a minimum of 10 working weeks between the deadline and the project start date.

Deadline: 29 Jul 2026

Creative Steps funds the professional, business and organisational development of artists who have experienced barriers to careers within the arts. Creative Steps for Individuals is open to individual artists and creatives who identify as being Ethnically and Culturally diverse, D/deaf, disabled or neurodivergent, groups that are underrepresented in Arts Council of Wales funding programmes due to barriers to entry. Applicants must have demonstrable experience as an artist or creative professional; those without may still be supported on the basis of artistic potential by submitting a portfolio, CV or examples of work, discussed with a Relationship Manager. WHAT IT SUPPORTS: career development, NOT creating new work or project delivery. Examples include peer-to-peer learning, training courses (not formal/vocational education), building networks, mentorship/coaching, business development/social media/marketing advice, website development/marketing materials/equipment or materials, and setting up a new organisation. Budgets may include the applicant's own time, freelancer costs (paid at standard industry rates), mentor/consultant costs, access costs, and childcare/caregiving costs. AMOUNT: GBP 500 to GBP 7,500 (plus access costs); up to 90% of the developmental cost, with the remaining 10% from a non-Arts Council of Wales / non-National Lottery source (in-kind, cash, or both, confirmed by letters of support). No application fee. APPLY: applicants must express interest at least 3 weeks before the deadline to receive support from an Application Coach or Access Support coordinator; an application form is provided by a Relationship Manager. The fund runs in rounds; the next round (Round 2 2026) has an Expression of Interest cut-off of 8 July 2026 and an application deadline of 29 July 2026. Later rounds: Round 3 (EOI 30 September, deadline 21 October 2026) and Round 4 (EOI 10 February, deadline 3 March 2027).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Project Funding for Individual Artists 2026 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland.

Award: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Project-based activity; duration not specified.

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

Project Funding for Individuals, part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds project-based creative activities across all artistic disciplines. Eligible activities include creating new work, research and development, residencies, mentoring, professional development, and time dedicated to specific creative outcomes. ELIGIBLE COSTS: art materials, artist fees/time, residency costs, travel (mileage at GBP 0.257/mile), venue hire, technical equipment (max 50% of request value), and IT equipment (laptops/PCs up to GBP 1,200; tablets up to GBP 500). ELIGIBILITY: artists of all disciplines and career stages, including emerging artists (less than 5 years professional experience), and in some cases technical staff/administrators; students and academics at third-level institutions may apply with departmental approval showing the costs are not covered by their employer or academic work. AMOUNT: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. No application fee. Deadline: Thursday 30 July 2026, 12:00 noon (online submission required).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Artists' International Development Fund 2026 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland; international development takes place abroad with overseas partners/hosts.

Award: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Early-stage international development opportunities; residencies supported for a maximum of 4 weeks.

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

The Artists' International Development Fund (AIDF), part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds early-stage international development opportunities for individual, freelance and self-employed artists to build networks abroad. ELIGIBLE COSTS: art materials, travel, residencies (up to 4 weeks), technical fees, venue hire, and equipment, with IT devices capped at GBP 1,200 for laptops/PCs and GBP 500 for tablets. ELIGIBILITY: artists across all disciplines, particularly emerging artists (under 5 years professional experience); academics and students at third-level institutions may apply if the project differs from their employment/studies. AIDF applicants must have received recognition for their work in Northern Ireland, lack extensive international experience, and demonstrate interest in their work from an overseas partner/host. AMOUNT: GBP 62 to GBP 15,000. No application fee. Deadline: Thursday 30 July 2026, 12:00 noon.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIAP: Major Individual Artists Award 2026/27 (Arts Council NI)
item.description:
Organisation: Arts Council of Northern Ireland

Location: Northern Ireland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be based in Northern Ireland.

Award: Up to GBP 15,000 per award; four awards available in 2026/27. Funded through the National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP).

Duration: Supports specific projects, specialised research, personal artistic development and materials/equipment. Activity duration not specified.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

The Major Individual Artists award, part of Arts Council NI's National Lottery Support for Individual Artists Programme (SIAP), funds extended or ambitious work by established artists, covering specific projects, specialised research, personal artistic development, and materials/equipment. AMOUNT: four awards of up to GBP 15,000 each in 2026/27. ELIGIBILITY: established artists across all disciplines with national/international recognition who have not previously received a major award; academics and postgraduate artists are eligible if the project costs do not relate to their institutional work or studies. Applicants must contact the relevant artform officer before submission (by 10 July 2026); applications without this prior contact are ineligible. No application fee. The online application system closes precisely at the deadline and applicants must receive a confirmation email to verify successful submission. Deadline: Monday 20 July 2026, 12:00 noon.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creative Scotland Open Fund for Individuals
item.description:
Organisation: Creative Scotland

Location: Scotland, United Kingdom. Applicants must be living in Scotland and hold a UK bank account in their own name.

Award: Between GBP 500 and GBP 50,000. Overall annual budget for the fund is GBP 5 million.

Duration: Supports research, development and/or delivery of creative activity (specific projects and productions) for up to 24 months.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Open Fund for Individuals is one of Creative Scotland's key funding programmes, supporting the wide range of activity initiated by artists, writers, producers and other creative practitioners in Scotland. The overall annual budget for this fund is GBP 5 million. WHAT IT SUPPORTS: periods of research, development and/or delivery of creative activity, such as specific projects and productions, for up to 24 months; it can support an individual's time where this is related to specific creative outcomes. Applicants are asked to give a start and end date for the activity and to describe the outcomes, benefits and impacts they wish to achieve. ELIGIBILITY: freelance and self-employed artists and creative practitioners living in Scotland who are at least 18 years old; applicants must have a UK bank account in their own name. Those who are not self-employed individuals or sole traders may instead be eligible for the National Lottery Open Fund for Organisations. FUNDING AMOUNTS: between GBP 500 and GBP 50,000. TIMESCALES: requests between GBP 500 and GBP 20,000 receive an outcome in about 10 weeks; requests between GBP 20,001 and GBP 50,000 in about 14 weeks. This is a rolling open fund with no fixed deadline. Download the guidance for applicants and apply via the online portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Freelands Foundation: Learning Through Making Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Freelands Foundation

Location: UK (England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, Wales); activity must occur within the United Kingdom.

Award: Up to £25,000. No application fee.

Duration: Projects of any length, taking place between 1 January and 31 December 2027. Funding provided no earlier than December 2026.

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

The Learning Through Making fund supports UK-based visual art organisations to bring opportunities for making and material exploration into their art education work. Freelands Foundation is looking for exploratory projects that embrace openness, collaboration, and iteration -- enabling organisations to work with materials in an expansive way, unpicking preconceptions about making and material literacy without predefined outcomes. ELIGIBLE ACTIVITIES: providing materials, transport, or access to expertise and facilities; action-research trials such as a new series of workshops or events developing material literacy; adding a making-focused element to existing activity. Projects may range from a few days to several months, but must take place between 1 January and 31 December 2027. AMOUNT: up to £25,000; funding provided no earlier than December 2026. ELIGIBILITY: UK-based visual art organisations run with a charitable purpose and for public benefit; eligible types include charities registered in England and Wales, Scotland, or Northern Ireland; exempt charities; galleries, museums, or organisations within a Local Authority or larger charitable body (e.g. universities); and Community Interest Companies or Community Benefit Societies meeting specific criteria (charitable purposes, asset lock, no dividend distribution, ability to segregate restricted funds). Organisations with a prior Freelands Foundation grant relationship are eligible; you may not apply for activity already funded by Freelands Foundation. Individuals are not eligible. HOW TO APPLY: download the Application Form, Application Guidance, and optional Access Form from freelandsfoundation.co.uk; email completed form to grants@freelandsfoundation.co.uk with subject line 'LTM Fund - Application'; one additional page of images or diagrams may be included. APPLICATION SUPPORT SESSIONS (online): 9 July 2026, 10:30am-12pm; 12 August 2026, 10:30am-12pm. KEY DATES: applications open 17 June 2026; close 11 September 2026 at 12pm; outcomes shared 23 November 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BFI Doc Society: Features Production Fund (UK, Documentary)
item.description:
Organisation: BFI Doc Society

Location: UK

Award: Up to £150,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Feature-length non-fiction films for theatrical release; rolling two-stage process (EOI then full application)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The BFI Doc Society Features Production Fund (delivered via BFI with Doc Society as delegate partner) supports UK filmmakers producing non-fiction feature films intended for theatrical release. Two strands: Directions (first- or second-time directors) and Momentum (established talent). AMOUNT: up to £150,000 non-recoupable grant (the total Doc Society contribution per project cannot exceed £150,000). ELIGIBILITY: UK-based filmmakers, both emerging and established directors; experienced and debut producers eligible; projects must meet at least three of six stated priorities: equity/diversity, audience impact, talent development, creative risk, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. PROCESS: two-stage — submit an Expression of Interest, then a Full Application if invited. Rolling intake year-round. Opened May 2026. APPLY via BFI Doc Society website. Contact: hellobfi@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 - Research Track
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI)

Location: London, UK; in-person, full-time.

Award: £12,000 salary for the three-month term, plus travel support to London and weekday lunch provided. Visa sponsorship (3-month temporary work visa) available. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, 18 January to 9 April 2027.

Deadline: Closed 12 Jul 2026

GovAI's UK Winter Fellowship (Research Track) is a three-month in-person fellowship in London for researchers and policy professionals seeking to enter or advance careers in AI governance and policy. Fellows conduct independent research on a topic of their choice, paired with a GovAI supervisor from the team or network; the first two weeks are spent exploring the AI governance landscape before a research proposal is finalised. Research outputs may include a report, white paper, journal article, op-ed, or blog post targeted at an audience relevant to AI governance. Alongside the project, fellows attend expert Q&As, weekly seminars, skills workshops, work-in-progress meetings, and social events, and are encouraged to discuss follow-on career opportunities with GovAI's team and network. GovAI alumni have gone on to roles in US, UK, and EU government; leading AI companies (Anthropic, DeepMind, OpenAI); top think tanks (CSET, RAND); and leading universities (Oxford, Cambridge). AMOUNT: £12,000 salary for three months, plus travel support to London and weekday lunch. Visa sponsorship for a 3-month temporary work visa is available; successful visa applicants must remain in their country of application while the visa is processed. ELIGIBILITY: open to a wide range of backgrounds (government, academia, industry, civil society); no strict degree requirements; relevant areas include public policy, political science, computer science, economics, biosecurity, cybersecurity, China studies, and risk management. Senior candidates who need accommodations should email fellowships@governance.ai with subject line 'senior candidate winter fellowship 2027' and a CV. SELECTION CRITERIA: quality and clarity of written work, relevant expertise, judgement in prioritising research directions, and team fit (intellectual honesty, openness to feedback, comfort with uncertainty). APPLICATION PROCESS: (1) written application and automated abstract reasoning assessment; (2) paid remote work test (August 2026); (3) remote interview and reference checks (September 2026); decisions communicated October 2026. Deadline: 12 July 2026, 23:59 BST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SOUND/IMAGE 2026: "Artistic Intelligence" - Call for Works
item.description:
Organisation: University of Greenwich

Location: University of Greenwich, London, UK; in-person attendance required.

Award: No payment to artists. All accepted artists must purchase a festival pass at a reduced rate of £25 (covers catering and technical support).

Duration: Festival runs 5-8 November 2026.

Deadline: Closed 19 Jun 2026

SOUND/IMAGE 2026 is the 11th edition of the University of Greenwich festival exploring sound and image practice, running 5-8 November 2026 in London. This year's theme is "Artistic Intelligence": a critical reflection on creative choice, agency, and authenticity in the context of algorithmic and AI-mediated art making. The festival cites reference points from Vera Molnár, Pauline Oliveros, John Cage, Iannis Xenakis, Ryoji Ikeda, and Refik Anadol. Special consideration is given to students, apprentices, and emerging artists; submissions are welcome from composers, filmmakers, artists, performers, and architects. CATEGORIES ACCEPTED: sound and music works; audiovisual works; talks; artist statements; workshops and practical demonstrations; live performances; installations. THEMATIC AREAS: embodied knowledge and lived experience in aesthetic judgement; creative choice vs chance encounters; ownership, authenticity, and AI co-creation; human and algorithmic capability; spatial and immersive audio-visual forms; interdisciplinary practice (CreaTech); practice research. SUBMISSION RULES: works under 15 minutes in duration (installations exempt from duration limit); maximum three submissions per artist across all categories; artists must attend and present in person; submit separately for each submission via Microsoft CMT. FESTIVAL PASS: all accepted artists must purchase a festival pass at a reduced rate of £25. Notifications before end of July 2026. Deadline: 19 June 2026, 23:59 UTC+1 (extended from 12 June). Submit at https://blogs.gre.ac.uk/sound-image/sound-image-2026-call-for-works/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BFI Doc Society: Made of Truth – Short Non-Fiction Film Fund (UK)
item.description:
Organisation: BFI Doc Society

Location: UK (both director and producer must be UK residents)

Award: Up to £25,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Short films 5–40 minutes; up to 13 projects funded per year

Deadline: Closed 01 Jul 2026

BFI Doc Society's Made of Truth fund supports early-career UK filmmakers making non-fiction live-action or animated short films of 5–40 minutes. Up to 13 projects are funded per annual round. AMOUNT: up to £25,000 per project. ELIGIBILITY: early-career directors and producers who are UK residents; teams must have at least two people (a producer and a director); directors cannot have received a commercial distribution deal for a non-fiction feature in the last five years; a reasonable proportion of early-career crew is required. Applications must meet at least three of six priorities: equity/diversity/inclusion, audience impact, talent development, creative risk, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. OPENS: 18 May 2026 at 1pm UK time. DEADLINE: 1 July 2026 at 1pm UK time. APPLY via the BFI Doc Society website. Contact: hellobfi@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BFI Doc Society: Expanded Screen Fund (UK, VR/AR/Immersive Non-Fiction)
item.description:
Organisation: BFI Doc Society

Location: UK (projects must demonstrate UK-wide benefit)

Award: £25,000–£150,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; feature-length or series immersive non-fiction works

Deadline: Closed 22 Jun 2026

The BFI Doc Society Expanded Screen Fund supports ambitious screen-based works of non-fiction intended to reach audiences beyond cinemas, including VR, AR, MR, and 360-degree experiences. Projects must be grounded in real events, locations, or narratives and must demonstrate a clear creative rationale for their technical approach. AMOUNT: £25,000–£150,000 non-recoupable grant. ELIGIBILITY: producers with a demonstrated track record in delivering immersive work; UK-based; projects must meet at least three of six stated priorities: equity/diversity/inclusion, audience impact, talent development, creative risk-taking, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. DEADLINE: 22 June 2026 at 1pm UK time. APPLY via the BFI Doc Society website. Contact: hellobfi@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
